context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the phoenix-liveview-essentials instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use this skill before writing ANY LiveView module or `.heex` template.

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Always add `@impl true`** before every callback (mount, handle_event, handle_info, render)

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Check `connected?(socket)`** before PubSub subscriptions, timers, or side effects

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `Map.get(assigns, :key, default)`** for optional assigns in helper functions

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `with` for error handling** in event handlers — assign errors to socket, don't crash

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Never use `auto_upload: true` with form submission** — use manual uploads instead

checklist.name: instruction-7
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Check `core_components.ex` for existing components** before creating custom ones

checklist.name: instruction-8
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Never query the database directly from LiveViews** — call context functions instead

checklist.name: instruction-9
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use streams for large collections** — see `liveview-streams` skill for details

checklist.name: instruction-10
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Define `mount/3`** — initialize all assigns with static defaults
